# UC-23.3.1 · Cross-Channel Marketing ROI and Multi-Touch Spend Versus Revenue

## Description

This monitoring pattern blends closed-won revenue and open pipeline from CRM opportunities with finance-tracked marketing spend so each normalized **campaign_key** row compares cash-out (often monthly by channel and region) to revenue credited through your chosen attribution spine — multi-touch linear or position-based allocations land upstream of Splunk as warehouse columns or replicated Marketo/Hybrid touches; operationally many teams still publish **last-touch** for pacing while revisiting **multi-touch** monthly. Unlike SEO-only traffic ratios or paid-media CPA dashboards tied to one ad platform, it deliberately crosses paid social, paid search, owned email, syndicated content, partner programs, events, and organic/direct spillover when CRM captures **LeadSource**, **Campaign**, or UTMs reconciled to the same taxonomy used in **marketing_spend_monthly**. Splunk surfaces ROI volatility when booking cadence (mid-month catch-up media invoices), attribution-window knobs (7 versus 180 days), or channel rename projects shift historical comparisons.

## Value

CMOs and marketing finance partners negotiate budget shifts using the same numerator (CRM-attributed pipeline and wins in functional currency) and denominator (cost-center spend aligned to campaigns) that appear in board packs — reducing shadow spreadsheets when Singapore spends USD-equivalent against EUR-booked pipeline during FX swings. Portfolio leadership gains an explicit lever on **spend_share_pct** versus **roi_pct** per **campaign_key**, making trade-offs visible between harvesting efficient channels and investing in lagged enterprise pursuits where nine-month cycles disconnect cash timing from closed revenue. Revenue operations can explain quarter-over-quarter swings using Splunk drilldowns tied to Salesforce Opportunity IDs rather than debating incompatible GA sessions.

## Implementation

Land CRM opportunities through DB Connect **jdbc_** inputs with watermark polling; ingest **marketing_spend.csv** or finance mart rows keyed by campaign and calendar month; optionally mirror UTM-bearing web hits via HEC for reconciliation dashboards; normalize taxonomy between CRM **campaign_source**, lookup rows, and finance dimensions before publishing Dashboard Studio boards owned jointly by demand generation and FP&A.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud roles allowing index creation (`business`, `web`), DB Connect administrative privileges (Splunkbase 2686), and KV/credential storage for JDBC passwords.
• Salesforce Enterprise/Unlimited or comparable CRM warehouse replica exposing Opportunity-grade facts — minimally amount, stage labels, campaign attribution fields, CreatedDate-style timestamps — refreshed frequently enough for leadership cadences.
• Finance-approved mapping between ERP/cost-center spend extracts (`marketing_spend_monthly`) and CRM taxonomy owners who rename campaigns quarterly.
• Python-not-required path preferred; rely on JDBC polling rather than bespoke Splunk SDK scripts unless Bulk API parity already exists elsewhere.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Provision three ingestion lanes so Splunk never confuses pacing dashboards with ERP truth.

Lane A (CRM revenue spine, Splunk DB Connect jdbc_): Build a JDBC identity using your Salesforce replica credentials with least privilege (`SELECT` on Opportunity tables only). Configure `inputs.conf`-merged stanza via DB Connect UI → Inputs → Database Inputs: SQL akin to `SELECT opportunity_id, stage_name, amount, currency_iso_code, created_date, close_date, campaign_source, lead_source, customer_segment, region, last_modified_date FROM crm.sfdc_opportunity WHERE last_modified_date > ?` with rising column `last_modified_date` and initial catch-up bounded to 400 days for B2B lag analysis. Emit to `index=business`, `sourcetype=dbx:crm_opportunities`, `_time` mapped from `last_modified_date` for near-real-time freshness while financial cut still uses CreatedDate math inside SPL. For orgs lacking a warehouse, mirror UC-23.2.1’s Bulk API 2.0 poller feeding `salesforce:opportunity` through HEC — the sample SPL unions that sourcetype.

Lane B (finance spend, DB Connect weekly): Separate JDBC connection string to Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift finance schema. Schedule `cron` weekly (for example Monday 06:00 local) after FP&A publishes accruals. SQL should return `campaign_source`, `month`, `spend_amount`, `currency_code`, `cost_center`, `region`, and optional `booking_batch` so mid-month adjustments can be audited. Emit `sourcetype=dbx:marketing_spend_monthly`. If warehouse access is blocked, publish `marketing_spend.csv` under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/search/lookups/` with read permissions for the search role and document the SharePoint workflow that updates rows — inputlookup still powers the SPL example.

Lane C (UTM telemetry sample, optional HEC): Forward Apache `access_combined` with `preserveLabel` on full query strings or ship GA4 BigQuery batch events with `event_params` flattened to top-level fields. Rate-limit heavy inputs; this lane is diagnostic to compare strip rates, not authoritative for ROI numerators. Store in `index=web`. Configure HEC tokens on a dedicated ingest port, separate from security-restricted indexes.

**Step 2 — Publish the Splunk logic and stewardship guardrails**
Save the SPL from the top-level `spl` field as a report `rpt_marketing_channel_roi_monthly`. Before sharing, clone a `macros.conf` stanza `default_attribution(create_month)` implementing the `case()` block you adopt in-house (for example align spend month to opportunity-create month rather than CloseDate for demand-gen steering). Explain the narrative: the initial `stats` pass rolls opportunity rows into one line per campaign_key × create_month so marketers read the same grain as finance accruals; the `join` pulls total_spend from the lookup or Mart; `fillnull` guards empty spend keys; `eventstats` adds portfolio denominators so spend_share_pct reveals concentration risk beyond single-channel ROI. Add a companion alert `alert_roi_collapse` firing when three-month rolling roi_pct drops more than twenty points while pipeline_to_spend holds — that pattern often precedes Creative fatigue on a single network, not Splunk breakage. Avoid reusing CPA-style alert text from paid-only programs — this watch covers blended cross-channel outcomes and should page demand-gen plus finance jointly. When large join cardinality appears, raise `max` and consider pre-summarizing spend in a summary index (`summary` `smry:marketing_spend`) with `sistats` nightly.

**Step 3 — Validate numbers before the CMO reviews them**
Pick five `OppId` values from Splunk results and locate them inside Salesforce Lightning → Opportunities with the same IDs, comparing `Amount`, `StageName`, and `LeadSource`. Compare against the Salesforce Lightning dashboard totals your RevOps team treats as authoritative on the same date slice — numbers should align within replication lag. In parallel, open HubSpot or Marketo deal or program reports if those systems feed the warehouse, verifying the campaign_key normalization table (sometimes a Google Sheet maintained by ops) matches each tool’s naming. Finance validation: reconcile `sum(total_spend)` for a quarter to your Workday Adaptive board package for demand generation; variances frequently trace to FX translation timing (EUR actuals booked at ECB month-end versus CRM USD functional) or delayed vendor invoices mapped to next month. For the optional web lane, compare UTM completeness with Adobe Analytics or GA4 explorations — if strip rates exceed twenty percent, capture the delta in the annotation field of the Dashboard Studio canvas. Document acceptable tolerances (for example ±1.5 percent on spend) inside the runbook so audits do not stall on rounding.

**Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and cadence without generic routing boilerplate**
Publish a Dashboard Studio workspace `db_mkt_roi_blended` with the visual mix from `visualization`: bar, bubble, funnel, area, singlevalue. Bind time and region tokens shared with RevOps so EMEA leadership does not inherit AMER fiscal calendars. Embed data quality callouts sourced from Splunk searches — e.g., distinct count of CRM rows with `campaign_key="unknown"` — to drive remediation in Marketo smart lists or Salesforce validation rules between reviews. Weekly demand-gen standups consume the blended view; monthly board prep snapshots export CSV from the same report to slide-deck partners, reducing cut/paste drift. Routing: low-priority Teams channel for informational weekly digests; escalate to VP Marketing and CFO office only when portfolio roi_pct drops below governance thresholds documented in Tableau of authorities. Forbid ad-hoc spreadsheet copies as system of record — finance must re-validate any manual tweak with a Jira change ticket referencing the Splunk saved search ID.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting deep dives when marketing math drifts**
• Month-end close shifts: If `dbx:marketing_spend_monthly` inserts late adjustments, rerun the DB Connect input with explicit `last_run` watermark or replay a targeted `dbquery` preview; Splunk `_indextime` minus `_time` skew over three days flags ETL regressions tied to FP&A edits. When total_spend looks missing entirely for a known-active quarter, open DB Connect Input Health in Splunk Web and confirm the JDBC poll returned rows rather than empty results after an auth failure.
• Attribution window changes: When leadership moves from ninety-day influence to one-hundred-eighty-day influence, recreate saved searches or macro parameters; expect roi_pct jumps of two to four times for cohort channels like display retargeting — document the before/after window in dashboard footers to avoid apples-to-oranges debates.
• Brand versus performance: Sudden uplift in organic or direct-attributed wins after a brand media flight is not Splunk ingestion noise; annotate the panel when out-of-home lift is not modeled so total_spend for non-UTM channels does not look wasteful relative to sourced revenue.
• Offline conversion lag: Google Offline Conversion Import or partner CAPI uploads two to four weeks after in-person meetings will depress early-month ROI until CRM updates; verify `last_modified_date` polling still captures those late field changes — if not, widen the JDBC predicate or schedule a one-time backfill.
• UTM strip sampling: Compare HEC-fed `access_combined` UTMs versus marketing automation redirect tags; if stripping exceeds agreed tolerance, escalate to web engineering and email operations (not Splunk admins alone) because ad blockers and click-wrappers alter query strings before events reach Splunk.
• Currency translation: Mismatched Amount currencies against spend_amount ledger lines show as wild `roi_pct` swings; enforce a `lookup` translating ISO codes using Treasury-published month-end rates and join on `YYYY-MM`.
• Multi-touch model drift: When marketing ops switches HubSpot revenue attribution models or Salesforce campaign influence weights, archive the prior Splunk macro version in Git so YoY Splunk dashboards reference consistent logic; note that model swaps can move credit without finance moving cash — separate “board metric” commentary from invoice actuals.
• Lead-to-opportunity latency: Large B2B organizations may see qualified demand land in CRM weeks after the originating touch; optional `streamstats` windowing in advanced searches can smooth pipeline_to_spend when create_month alignment under-represents late-stage accelerations.
• Sales-cycle misalignment: If account executives reclassify campaign_key near close to protect comp, expect CRM rows to jump between channels; join to OpportunityFieldHistory extracts if disputes arise, and freeze campaign tags after stage three to stop thrash.

This step set keeps the Splunk deployment aligned with marketing finance reality — not merely a replication of vendor ROAS screenshots — and preserves the sibling boundary: organic-traffic-only and per-platform CPA deep dives remain UC-23.3.4 and UC-23.3.5 respectively while this anchor holds the blended portfolio story.


## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities" OR sourcetype="salesforce:opportunity") earliest=-180d@d
| eval OppId=coalesce(opportunity_id, Id, id, OpportunityId)
| eval StageName=coalesce(StageName, stage_name)
| eval Amount=tonumber(coalesce(Amount, amount, "0"))
| eval campaign_key=lower(trim(coalesce(campaign_source, Campaign_Source, utm_campaign, lead_source, LeadSource, "Unknown")))
| eval IsClosedWon=if(like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(IsWon,is_won,""))),"^(1|true)$"),1,0)
| eval IsClosedF=if(like(StageName,"%Closed Won%") OR like(StageName,"%Closed Lost%") OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(IsClosed,is_closed,""))),"^(1|true)$"),1,0)
| eval CreateStr=coalesce(CreatedDate, created_date)
| eval create_ts=if(isnotnull(CreateStr) AND match(CreateStr,"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}"), strptime(replace(mvindex(split(CreateStr,"T"),0),"Z",""),"%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval create_month=strftime(create_ts,"%Y-%m")
| eval won_amt=if(IsClosedWon=1, Amount, 0)
| eval pipe_amt=if(IsClosedF=0, Amount, 0)
| stats sum(won_amt) as revenue_closed sum(pipe_amt) as pipeline_generated dc(OppId) as deals by campaign_key, create_month
| join type=left campaign_key create_month max=50000 [
    | inputlookup marketing_spend.csv
    | eval campaign_key=lower(trim(coalesce(campaign_source, channel, "Unknown")))
    | eval month_norm=if(match(month,"^\\d{4}-\\d{2}"), month, strftime(strptime(month,"%m/%d/%Y"),"%Y-%m"))
    | stats sum(spend) as total_spend by campaign_key month_norm
    | rename month_norm as create_month
]
| fillnull value=0 total_spend
| eventstats sum(total_spend) as portfolio_spend sum(revenue_closed) as portfolio_revenue
| eval roi_pct=if(total_spend>0, round((revenue_closed-total_spend)/total_spend*100,2), null())
| eval cost_per_deal=if(deals>0, round(total_spend/deals,2), null())
| eval pipeline_to_spend=if(total_spend>0, round(pipeline_generated/total_spend,3), null())
| eval spend_share_pct=if(portfolio_spend>0, round(100*total_spend/portfolio_spend,1), null())
| sort - roi_pct
| table campaign_key create_month total_spend deals pipeline_generated revenue_closed roi_pct cost_per_deal pipeline_to_spend spend_share_pct
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio first: horizontal **splunk.bar** of **roi_pct** by **campaign_key** trellised by **region**; paired **splunk.bubble** with X axis **total_spend**, Y axis **revenue_closed**, bubble diameter **deals**, color **customer_segment** from CRM joins; **splunk.funnel** showing counts from influenced pipeline rows through closed-won for the selected portfolio slice (distinct from funnel-stage conversion-rate UC **UC-23.3.2**); optional **splunk.area** stacked area for **spend_share_pct** across channels month-over-month; executive strip with **splunk.singlevalue** tiles for blended portfolio ROI and worst-quarter channel.
